pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh (line 65) explicitly references username 'mchen_parent' for cleanup; export_result.sh (line 24) queries for this username in login_authentication table. This is a synthetic identifier for task testing.
Code analysis: setup_task.sh (line 69) references 'Margaret' in staff table cleanup; export_result.sh (line 31) queries for Margaret Chen in staff table. This is a synthetic test name.
Code analysis: Task description and all scripts reference Chen as the last name for both parent Margaret and student Kevin. This is a synthetic test identifier.

Database schema definition: examples/opensis_env/scripts/direct_db_setup.sh (lines 83-89) defines user_profiles table with: (4, 'parent', 'Parent'). Confirmed by verifier.py (line 62) checking for profile_id == '4' for parent accounts.

Code analysis: setup_task.sh (lines 33-42) explicitly creates student with first_name='Kevin' if not exists; export_result.sh (line 15) references kevin_id from setup. This is a synthetic student account for task testing.
Code analysis: setup_task.sh (line 41) queries for student WHERE first_name='Kevin' AND last_name='Chen'; export_result.sh (line 50) references the same student. Synthetic test data.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses OpenSIS (an open-source Student Information System using PHP/MySQL) version 9.2 for automated testing. Profile ID 4 in the OpenSIS database system uniquely identifies user accounts with the 'Parent' role. All names and identifiers in this task (Margaret Chen as parent user mchen_parent; Kevin Chen as student) are synthetic test fixtures created during task setup for automated verification. The task involves creating a parent portal account and linking it to a student record within a fresh OpenSIS database instance.
